title: jacquemontia selloi var. rufescens
intro: Jacquemontia selloi var. rufescens, also known as red starvine or red morning glory, is a climbing vine native to North America, characterized by its reddish-brown stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Jacquemontia selloi var. rufescens include reducing inflammation, improving skin tone, and fighting off infections. The plant is traditionally prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
